author: @Zeero3846
text: My first time seeing these were in Chinese restaurants, where it was a giant backlit image of some Chinese waterfalls and rivers. They used this technique to make the water features in the image look like they were moving. To prevent the whole image from looking like there were long bars moving through it, they masked those areas off. I think they were lit from both the front and back to get the whole thing lit.

replies.author: @Zeero3846
replies.text: This was also in the area before true flat TVs, when "flat" actually just referred to the screen surface, not the entire unit, which was still a huge voluminous box.

author: @robertcraven4729
text:
Experiment worth considering?

With all the interest in diesel heaters, especially in camper vans etc.  I wonder if it would be possible to repurpose a diesel heater as a stove and / or oven / air frier.  What do you reckon.
